spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the differences between desktop hover constraints and mobile pointerdown spec boundaries in detail.

interactive.rationale: Allows interactive hovering/clicking/tapping on two device simulation blocks, printing instant telemetry and event logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Accurately defines the 138 optimization allowing touchstart/pointerdown to arm moderate eagerness speculation rules.

edge_cases.rationale: Nicely maps both desktop (hover triggers) and mobile (pointerdown triggers) pathways separately for visual clarity.

references.rationale: Links correctly to the ChromeStatus entry `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5079361929347072`.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared design elements, custom layouts, and clear colored badges.

openQuestions.title: Add touch latency visualization
openQuestions.detail: It would be beneficial to show a visual timer/bar indicating the exact millisecond difference between pointerdown and click events, since touch-latency-bench covers it, a small indicator here would tie the concepts nicely.

summary: A brilliant side-by-side simulator showing desktop hover triggers versus mobile touchstart/pointerdown, fully illustrating the 138 interop optimization.
